executive_summary.verdict: GM has NEGATIVE DCF for 10 consecutive years! This is a major red flag.
executive_summary.key_insight: DCF negative for 10 years: -$6 to -$16. Market knows something we don't.

graham.recommendation: 16% below Graham - but Graham alone is not enough

peter_lynch.recommendation: Lynch = 0 for auto companies (cyclical, no consistent growth)

10Y.analysis: Not a single positive DCF year. This is extremely rare and concerning.

dcf.recommendation: NEGATIVE DCF = Cash flows don't support current valuation

why_dcf_negative.capital_intensive: Auto manufacturing requires massive ongoing capex
why_dcf_negative.ev_transition: Billions invested in EV with uncertain returns
why_dcf_negative.cyclicality: Revenue and profits swing wildly with economy
why_dcf_negative.legacy_costs: Pension and healthcare obligations weigh on FCF

risk_factors.ev_transition: Spending billions on EVs that may not be profitable
risk_factors.competition: Tesla, Chinese EVs, legacy automakers all competing

bull_case.thesis: EV transition succeeds. Cruise (autonomous) becomes valuable. Below book value.

bear_case.thesis: EV money pit. Recession. Chinese competition. Legacy costs.

key_warning: 10 years of NEGATIVE DCF is a massive red flag. Only for speculators.
